diagrams.description: Shows the complete RSA algorithm flow from key generation through encryption to decryption, with all mathematical formulas at each step.
elements: Key Gen: p,q → n=pq, φ=(p-1)(q-1); Choose e, gcd(e,φ)=1; Compute d=e⁻¹ mod φ; Public: (e,n), Private: (d,n)
Proof: M^(ed) ≡ M (mod n) by Euler's theorem
diagrams.purpose: Complete RSA algorithm flow for exam reference
diagrams.title: Complete RSA Algorithm Flowchart

diagrams.description: Comparison of security levels: RSA key sizes vs symmetric key equivalents, with recommended key sizes for different security timeframes.
diagrams.title: RSA Key Size Security Comparison

diagrams.description: Shows the modular exponentiation process using square-and-multiply algorithm, demonstrating how to compute large powers modulo n efficiently.
elements: Process: if exponent bit=1: result=result×base; base=base² mod n; exponent>>=1
diagrams.purpose: Understanding modular exponentiation for RSA operations
diagrams.title: Square-and-Multiply Algorithm
